original_hypothesis.title: MagicLP ratioSync() can be permanently unusable if either reserve is zero (division by zero), hindering recovery workflows
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ratioSync() rescales targets proportionally to balance/reserve ratios using division by _BASE_RESERVE_ and _QUOTE_RESERVE_. If either reserve is zero while the corresponding balance is non-zero (or if balance differs), ratioSync() will revert due to division by zero, preventing the admin from using ratioSync as an operational tool to correct accounting after unexpected balance movements.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if reaching zero reserve is rare, Phase A invariants highlight dead-end states when a reserve hits zero. Having an admin recovery function that itself can revert in these states increases the risk of prolonged outages.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _BASE_TARGET_ = (_BASE_TARGET_ * baseBalance) / _BASE_RESERVE_; and similar for quote; no guard for _BASE_RESERVE_==0 or _QUOTE_RESERVE_==0

preconditions: Corresponding balance != reserve so the division branch executes

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Admin recovery-like function assumes non-zero reserves; dead-end state blocks it
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: after pool gets into zero-reserve state

operational_questions: Can zero-reserve states occur without malicious admin actions (rounding, extreme swaps, token behavior)?
Is ratioSync part of any documented ops runbook for pool recovery?
Should ratioSync fall back to _resetTargetAndReserve when reserve is zero?
validation_requirements: Property test: drive pool to _BASE_RESERVE_==0 with totalSupply>0 and attempt ratioSync
Operational drill: validate whether setParameters is the only viable recovery path

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Create conditions causing one reserve to hit zero (edge-case trading/withdrawal or non-standard token behavior)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Observe inability of operators to use ratioSync to recover targets
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Pool remains in degraded/bricked state until heavier admin intervention

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: recovery tooling failure; prolonged pool downtime

investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated failed admin attempts to repair pool

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
